facts.text: Butler Beach was founded, developed, and named after Frank B. Butler, a local entrepreneur who opened the first beach resort there.
During the 1940s, Butler Beach blossomed into a popular seaside destination for vacationers traveling along Florida’s Atlantic coast.
The resort built by Butler was the only beach open to African Americans between Jacksonville and Daytona Beach during segregation.
Today the former resort site is a stop on the St. Johns County Black History Trail, honoring its role in African‑American beach history.
Butler Beach sits on the Atlantic Ocean, just north of the Matanzas River, giving it a unique coastal geography that shaped its early development.
Frank B. Butler’s grocery store success enabled him to become a political activist, using his influence to secure beach access for marginalized communities.
